name: Can flooring be installed over a concrete slab in coastal homes?

mainEntity.name: Can new flooring be installed over a concrete slab in coastal homes in San Bruno or South San Francisco?
mainEntity.text: Can new flooring be installed over a concrete slab in coastal homes in San Bruno or South San Francisco?

acceptedAnswer.text: Concrete slabs can absolutely be used for flooring installation, but only if the slab is in good condition. Before installing, the contractor should confirm that the slab is dry enough and flat enough for the product being used. Moisture is the biggest concern because concrete can release vapor even when it looks dry. Depending on the flooring type, the installer may use a moisture barrier, underlayment, adhesive system, or self-leveling compound. Floating floors and certain vinyl products are often easier to install over slabs, while engineered wood and glued-down products may require more preparation and stricter moisture limits. In coastal areas, slab moisture can be affected by weather, irrigation, and the age of the foundation. If the slab has cracks, high spots, or low spots, those issues should be corrected before installation. Ask the installer what test they use, what the acceptable readings are, and whether the flooring warranty remains valid over the slab condition.
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, flooring can be installed over concrete slab if the slab is dry, flat, and compatible with the flooring system. The installer may need moisture testing, vapor protection, and leveling before using vinyl, tile, engineered wood, or glue-down products.
